title: Add Back to Home and Clear buttons to Content Generator panel

requirements.summary: Add a 'Back to Home' button in the Content Generator page that navigates to the home screen with TanStack Router
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Back to Home' (بازگشت به صفحه اصلی) button is visible on the Content Generator page.
Clicking the button navigates the user to the home screen (`/`) using TanStack Router.
The button styling matches the existing luxury dark/gold theme and does not interfere with other UI elements.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Back to Home' (بازگشت به صفحه اصلی) button near the top or bottom of the Content Generator panel. Import useNavigate from TanStack Router and implement onClick handler that navigates to '/'. Style the button consistently with the luxury dark/gold theme using existing Tailwind classes (e.g., bg-gold-600, hover:bg-gold-700, text-navy-900). Position the button so it does not interfere with existing input fields, generate button, or output areas.

requirements.summary: Add a 'Clear' button that resets all input fields, file uploads, generated content output, progress indicator state, and error messages to initial empty state
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Clear' (پاک کردن) button is visible on the Content Generator page, positioned near the input area or the generated output.
Clicking 'Clear' resets the URL input field to empty.
Clicking 'Clear' resets the free-text input area to empty.
Clicking 'Clear' removes any uploaded file previews and clears the file input state.
Clicking 'Clear' removes the generated content output (ContentOutput panel, ProductUploadHelper panel) from view.
Clicking 'Clear' resets the multi-stage progress indicator to its initial hidden/idle state.
Clicking 'Clear' removes any displayed error or warning messages.
After clicking 'Clear', the URL input field receives focus so the user can immediately type a new link.
The button is styled consistently with the luxury dark/gold theme and is visually distinct from the 'Generate Content' button.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Clear' (پاک کردن) button positioned near the input area or generated output. Implement a handleClear function that resets all local React state: URL input (empty string), free-text input (empty string), uploaded file state (null or empty), generated content output (null or hidden), multi-stage progress indicator state (reset to initial/idle), and any error/warning message state (empty string or null). After clearing all state, programmatically focus the URL input field using a ref. Style the button to be visually distinct from the 'Generate Content' button (e.g., use a secondary or outline style with gold/navy colors). Ensure the clear action does not trigger a page reload—all resets are in-memory state updates only.
